reasoning: The baseline is the actual index value for 2026-01-18, which corresponds most closely to the preceding Jan 14 observation of 2727.1 (since the series is weekly ending Wednesday). Over the past two weeks, C&I loans experienced two uncharacteristically large positive spikes (+9.4 and +9.0 billion). Historical weekly behavior in this series over the last three months (such as in late Nov and late Dec) clearly demonstrates that large jumps of ~+9 billion are usually followed swiftly by negative mean reversions (e.g., drops of -7 to -8 billion). Because the Jan 14 underlying baseline starts at a significant positive outlier, short-term trends heavily favor a mean-reverting drop for the subsequent observation on Jan 21 (which corresponds to the Jan 25 horizon). I estimate the probability of a further increase at 0.36. For the later resolution target (Feb 17), the series will have another month to benefit from its baseline positive macroeconomic drift (~+1.3 bn/week or +5 bn/month), which should partially or wholly compensate for any near-term mean-reverting drop. Accordingly, the probability converges closer to a coin flip, estimated at 0.46, accurately fulfilling the instruction that forecast uncertainty should increase with horizon.
